pass1_value.note: Developers can register an application in the developer console to obtain client_id and client_secret instantly after logging in; no mention of paid-only or partner gating.

final_value.note: The Applications documentation states that to use the Podio API you must create an application, and when you create an application Podio generates a client_id and client_secret; it also states you can create an application from the Developer Portal after logging in (self-serve access to credentials).

pass1_value.note: API docs describe a RESTful HTTP API covering many resource types (items, apps, tasks, users, orgs, spaces, contacts, etc.), with CRUD operations.

final_value.note: The Podio API documentation states the Podio API is a RESTful API over HTTP with JSON responses, and lists multiple resource areas (e.g., organizations, spaces, apps, items, tasks, users, contacts), indicating a broad REST API surface.

pass1_value.note: Public REST API docs plus self-serve client credentials are available; developers can register apps and use OAuth flows to obtain tokens and call endpoints.

final_value.note: Official developer docs confirm a RESTful API endpoint at https://api.podio.com and self-serve creation of an application in the Developer Portal to obtain client_id/client_secret, supporting implementability today.
